script:
Chapter 1 

"Ms. Savage, your request for a luxury cruise and a contractual mate is ... well, demanding. We'll need at least a month to find someone suitable."

The manager, Andy Stone, dressed in a tailored black suit, kept his voice low and careful. A silver wolf-head emblem gleamed coldly at his collar—symbol of the Nocturne Order, an elite agency that catered exclusively to the upper ranks of werewolf society.

As the future Lunaris Pack's Alpha, Ceanna Savage exuded an aura of dominance that made even seasoned professionals instinctively cautious around her.

Female Alpha heirs were almost unheard of across the Northmere Continent, and she was already commanding respect long before officially ascending to power.

Her engagement to Sirius Lowell, Alpha of the Astral Pack, was supposed to seal an alliance that would push both packs into a new golden era. Their official marking ritual was just a month away.

But clearly, Ceanna wasn't thrilled about the arrangement. Just days ago, she'd approached the Nocturne Order with an outrageous request: find her a new contractual mate.

"Two weeks. Double pay. If you can't do it, I'll find someone who can."

There was no room for argument in her voice—it crackled with the natural command of a born Alpha.

"Well, I suppose it's not impossible; please sign here," Andy stammered, surrendering under pressure. His fingernails lengthened into claws as he pressed his signature onto the enchanted contract parchment—an official mark of authentication in werewolf magic.

"Fifty percent down payment. We guarantee confidentiality and results," he added, forcing a professional smile.

Ceanna reached for the quill, her hand trembling slightly as her wolf, Allie, stirred violently inside her.

"Ceanna, are you out of your mind?!" Allie howled in her head. "You and Sirius grew up together! The two of you are childhood sweethearts, and he's your destined mate, chosen by the Moon Goddess herself! You have to trust him; you can't condemn him over a few pictures!"

"Enough! Shut up!" Ceanna cursed shrilly, frowning as she sensed Allie's stirring violently inside her body.

She signed without hesitation. The moment the quill touched the parchment, ancient runes glowed blue across the contract—proof that the magical seal had taken effect.

She paid the deposit in full, then walked out of the building.

The moment she stepped onto the curb, the roar of an approaching engine sliced through the air. A red Ferrari—its body emblazoned with a wolf totem—screeched to a stop in front of her.



Chapter 2
The headlights didn't cast ordinary beams, but wolf-shaped shadows—an unmistakable mark of a high-ranking Alpha.

"Ceanna," Sirius called as he pushed open the door, "I'm here to take you to the dress fitting."

Sirius silver-streaked hair shimmered in the sunlight as he walked over with confident strides. Without warning, he pulled her into his arms, a low growl vibrating through his chest.

But right now, the sight of him made Ceanna's stomach churn.

Today, he wore a suit—rare for him. The look was clean and handsome, a far cry from his usual wild, rebel vibe.

But whether he was in a tux or torn jeans, Sirius always radiated the same magnetic alpha force.

"People are watching; let go of me," Ceanna whispered, trying to wriggle out of his arms. Her voice had already started to roughen with partial transformation.

"Let them watch," Sirius murmured. "I want the whole world to know you're mine. My future Luna."

He kissed her on the forehead and released a wave of his pheromones over her—a territorial gesture no one in their world could mistake.

Gasps and excited murmurs erupted around them. "Look, it's our alpha!"

"Oh my gosh, he's so in love with his mate!"

"I heard he proposed to her under the full moon, right in front of the Moon Goddess statue, with tens of thousands of wolves watching! And he gave her the Noctra—the Astral Pack's sacred heirloom!"

"To be his Luna ... she must be the luckiest woman alive."

Several young she-wolves nearby began to blush uncontrollably, their furry ears popping out as the sweet scent of their pheromones thickened the air in the wake of the Alpha's presence.

A few bolder admirers rushed over to take photos with them.

Sirius, who was usually known for being ruthless and cold, now held Ceanna gently and posed with the patience of a gentleman.

And Ceanna, standing in the heart of Astral Pack territory, had no choice but to smile and flash a peace sign beside him.

But beneath the sugary air of admiration, she caught a faint, bitter tang in her chest.

Was she really the luckiest, Luna?

She used to believe that.


Chapter 3

At 12, when her mother—broken by her destined mate's betrayal—tried to take both their lives by lighting a thorn pyre soaked in wolfsbane, it was Sirius who saved her. He'd sensed the poison in the air and raced across pack borders to pull her out from the sealed room.

At 14, her stepmother hired rogues to murder Ceanna and strip her of her alpha potential. Sirius had come crashing in, transformed into a silver giant wolf, tearing through the rogues to protect her—only to be poisoned by the wolfsbane laced into the scene. He spent a month recovering.

At 18, the moment he completed his coming-of-age ceremony and became Astral Pack's new alpha, he publicly claimed Ceanna as his mate. Sirius vowed to wait until she came of age before marking her.

Their mothers had been best friends—the Lunas of their respective packs—and had agreed on their marriage since they were toddlers. Sirius's mother, Miriam Lowell, treated Ceanna like her own daughter—sometimes better than her son.

And Ceanna had believed—truly believed—that she'd be happy forever.

An envelope full of explicit photos arrived that same night he proposed.

And the girl in the pictures? Paige Reed—her stepsister. A powerless Omega.

Ceanna had drunk herself into oblivion, nearly dying from the alcohol. Even now, she couldn't understand—why would Sirius betray her? 


The most luxurious bridal boutique in the Astral Pack glimmered under the midday sun; the silver wolf-head totem above the entrance flickered in the light. Dozens of uniformed staff stood in perfect formation outside, ready to welcome their Alpha and future Luna.

As the future Luna, Ceanna was surrounded like a queen, escorted through three extravagant floors filled with wedding gowns.

"Luna, our Alpha truly adores you. Every diamond sewn into these dresses was mined and personally cut by him. Even the threads were soaked in the sacred waters of the Moon Goddess Temple," one of the staff gushed eagerly, barely pausing for breath.

In the corner, Sirius sat sprawled on a velvet couch, tapping at his phone with his clawed fingers. His silver tail twitched restlessly, sweeping across the cushions and sending fine silver fur into the air.

He accidentally pressed something; a sultry female voice blared from the phone's speaker.

"Sirius ... " Sirius's wolf ears sprang up in alarm as the seductive voice echoed in the room.



Chapter 4

Fumbling, he jabbed at the screen to silence it, but accidentally sliced the phone screen with a claw in his haste. Ceanna, standing not far away, had heard it. She didn't react, not even blink. Instead, she calmly returned to pick up her wedding dress, pretending she hadn't heard a thing.

Only then did Sirius breathe out, visibly relieved. His stiff tail slowly dropped, and he stood up in a rush, scrambling for an excuse.

"I—I need to use the restroom."

Ceanna didn't look at him but grunted an approval, though every inch of her bristled from that one whispered name.

"I'll browse on my own," she told the staff. "You can go back to work. If I need anything, I'll call," she said casually, waving off the staff.

Casually, she wandered the floor.

Upstairs, one of the changing room doors on the third floor was left slightly ajar. Ceanna's wolf ears twitched—catching faint, rhythmic sounds behind it.

She sniffed the air. A thick scent of lust clung to the space around her.

"Motherfucker!" Allie howled inside her. "They're really doing it. Just like in those photos!

"Ceanna, you were right! I kept telling myself it was just a one-time mistake—but no! They're really doing this behind our backs! How could he do this to us? We're his destined mate!"

Her voice cracked, trembling with rage and heartbreak. "I can't accept this. I won't! Tear them apart, Ceanna—use your claws and rip them to shreds!" Allie's fury surged; she had partially transformed. Glowing silver patterns pulsed over Ceanna's arms and neck, like runes carved into living flesh.

Ceanna clenched her fists so tightly that her claws broke through her skin, drawing blood. No one wanted revenge more than she did. No one didn't want to drag those two cheaters into the light and tear them apart. But the last thread of rationality whispered a cruel reminder: Leave them a shred of dignity. Don't stoop to their level.

From inside the changing room, Paige's voice floated out again, laced with heat only recognizable to wolves during mating season. "Sirius... who looks better in the wedding dress—me or Ceanna?" Sirius's snarl came sharp and unfiltered. "Shut up. Focus. Stop talking."

Ceanna froze. She turned her head toward a nearby mirror—and instantly regretted it. The reflection captured everything in grim, humiliating detail. That was enough. Her stomach lurched violently as the scene reflected back at her.


Chapter 5
Sirius's silver tail whipped back and forth in agitation. His hands roughly spun Paige around, pinning her against the wall. His claws ripped the strapless wedding dress from her body in one swift motion.

The sound of tearing fabric mixed with his uncontrolled growls, each sound stabbing into Ceanna's chest like a dagger.

The mirror captured everything. He entered her roughly from behind. The motion. The betrayal. It was all laid bare.

"No, I want you to answer me. Who looks better—me or her? Who makes you happier? Is it me or was it her?"

"It's you. Of course it's you."

Sirius's voice, low and raspy, was thick with pleasure he couldn't hide.

Ceanna's hand flew to her chest. It felt like a thousand arrows had pierced her heart at once. Without a word, she turned and descended the stairs. She was consumed by pain, her tail dragging behind her like a banner of defeat.

"Luna, did anything catch your eye?" a staff member called out, hurrying toward her. "I can bring it out for you."

Ceanna didn't lift her head. "Could you bring me a glass of water?" she asked softly.

Her voice was calm; she struggled to stay composed. She tucked her claws away, forced her tail into stillness, and sat gracefully on the sofa—like nothing had just shattered her world upstairs.

Then, casually, she pulled out her phone and dialed Sirius.

No answer.

She called again.

This time, he picked up.

"Ceanna? What's up?"

"Where are you?"

"Just outside. Having a smoke. I'll be right back." His voice shook—barely controlled.

"Ahh!"

Paige let out a soft, deliberately provocative moan—the kind only a she-wolf in heat could make. But before the sound could fully escape, Sirius's hand clamped tightly over her mouth.

The line went dead, just like that.

Ceanna hung up the call. She didn't say a word. Like an ostrich burying its head in the sand, she lowered her face, hoping that if she curled in far enough, she could hide all the shame, all the heartbreak, from the world.

"What do you think you're doing? How many times have I told you? Whatever's going on between us—Ceanna can never find out. If she does, I swear, I'll kill you." After indulging himself, Sirius's claws tightened around Paige's chin. His pupils darkened, flaring crimson as he growled a warning through gritted teeth.


Chapter 6
"Ow! That hurts! I know." Paige pouted, but instead of pulling away, she gripped his hand.

Her fingers wrapped around his hand. Slowly, she brought it to her lips and licked it. She was seductive, playful, and dangerous.

"You're such a slut, Paige," Sirius growled, his voice rough and fevered. "That's what I love about you." Then he kissed her—fierce and possessive—as though he wanted to consume her entirely.

Suddenly, a scream erupted from downstairs, followed by frantic footsteps.

"Oh my gosh! Fire! Someone help—there's a fire! The wedding gowns have caught fire!"

One of the staff, on her way to deliver Ceanna's water, had spotted smoke curling up from the display and shouted in panic.

Downstairs, Ceanna didn't even flinch. She just sat there on the velvet sofa, staring blankly ahead as row after row of snow-white wedding gowns went up in flames. Her golden eyes slowly shifted, darkening into a deep, crimson hue.

Wolves, by nature, feared fire. But Ceanna was no ordinary wolf. As a future Alpha, she didn't fear fire—she commanded it. 

"Ceanna! Ceanna! What are you standing there for? This place is on fire! Get out of here; it's not safe!"

The moment he caught wind of the fire, Sirius shifted into his wolf form. With a burst of power only a werewolf could summon, he crashed through the third-story window and landed on the pavement below.

"Our wedding dress is gone; we can't get married now," Ceanna murmured with a bittersweet smile.

Her eyes shimmered with unshed tears as she bit down hard on her lower lip, trying not to let her tears flow down.

"We'll order a new one," Sirius said quickly. "Nothing can stop our wedding." Before she could answer, he scooped her up into his arms and teleported her out of the burning boutique.

But in that fleeting moment—cradled against his chest—Ceanna caught a scent, which only lingered after mating.

She had always believed that no matter how reckless Sirius could be, he would protect her above all else in a crisis.

But outside, her world came crashing down. Paige was already in the front passenger seat.

Her bare shoulders were covered in hickeys, her lips swollen from kisses, and her eyes glinted with mocking pride—flaunting her victory of how intense the two of them got earlier on.

Ceanna didn't need anyone to explain. She had lost. Completely, devastatingly lost.



Chapter 7
Inside her, Allie howled anguishly, her fury dissolving into disappointment. "Ceanna, they'd cheat behind our backs. Now they're flaunting it like it's a badge of honor. I guess we really don't matter to him. So much for being destined mates."

When a destined mate betrays the bond, it doesn't just break hearts—it wounds the soul. The wolf spirit can shatter from the pain, and in some cases, even die.

Allie had held on until now, refusing to believe Sirius would betray them. But seeing it with her own eyes, her strength caved in like a crumbling cliff.

Energy drained from her body in an instant, and she collapsed within Ceanna, unable to even stand.

Ceanna felt the shift immediately, panic rising in her throat. "Hold on, Allie, just a little longer. We'll leave this place. We'll find a new mate. I promise."

A single tear slipped down her cheek. She couldn't help wondering—what had she done to deserve this?

She had told him from the start—if he didn't love her, he could be honest.

But what she couldn't forgive was being made into a fool.

"Ceanna, Paige was nearby and just needed a ride. Paige, move to the backseat. Ceanna sits here," Sirius ordered, pulling open the door and yanking Paige roughly by the arm.

His claws scraped her skin, leaving a bloody mark on her forearm.

"Sirius, I twisted my ankle while shopping. It hurts so badly! Can you take me to the hospital? Ceanna won't mind, right?" Paige whined, her hands clasped together like a pleading child.

Her tail flicked smugly behind her, quietly displaying how proud she was.

"How about this? I'll take her to the ER, and you can get a cab back."

Sirius didn't wait for an answer. He was already calling a taxi, completely ignoring Ceanna's feelings.

Too drained to argue, Ceanna climbed silently into the cab.

Inside, Allie growled from deep within her, "Motherfucker! Screw those traitors!"

She didn't hear anything Sirius said after that. All she could see in her mind was the inferno that devoured the boutique.

Once Ceanna was in the taxi, Sirius slammed the door shut and sped off with Paige beside him.

As they reached a deserted spot by the river, Sirius suddenly hit the brakes. The tires screeched, dust curling up around them.

"Was it you, Paige?" he growled. "Did you start that fire? Were you trying to kill Ceanna?" He turned to her, his claws pressed tightly to her throat. As the mighty Alpha, he could end an Omega's life in a heartbeat.


Chapter 8
Sirius radiated a power so suffocating, the weight of it alone might crush Paige to death.

"No! It wasn't me! I was with you—how could I have started a fire?" Paige whimpered, her wolf ears drooping, her tail slapping weakly against his arm, and attempted to break free from him.

"It better not be," Sirius growled, his voice low and dangerous. "If I find out you've been playing games behind my back, I will end you."

"Why, Sirius? Why her?" Paige sobbed, her voice raw. "What does Ceanna have that I don't? Sure, she's the heiress to the pack, but I'm the Alpha's daughter too! If it's about a political match, I could've been that! I've been with you since I was 17. I've given you pleasure she never could. So why can't it be me?"

Her words broke into desperate little whimpers—the kind an Omega made when her life was in danger. It was a biological plea for mercy.

But Paige knew, deep down, that it all came down to one thing: Ceanna was his destined mate. That bond gave her a power Paige could never touch. Ceanna didn't need to fight for Sirius's attention—he gave it to her freely, unconditionally.

"Shut up," Sirius snapped. "Don't you dare compare yourself to Ceanna. You're not even in the same league. Ceanna is my destined mate—the future Luna of my pack. She was born to be loved. You? You were just a distraction. If you're not happy with that, walk away. I've never been short on women."

He gripped her chin harshly, his fingers pressing deep into her bones, as if ready to crush it. It wasn't just a warning—it was a threat wrapped in fury.

But Paige clung to him like a drowning girl to driftwood. "I don't want to break up," she whispered, her voice trembling. "I know you don't love me. But I love you. I can't live without you. I'll do whatever you say. I won't challenge Ceanna again. I promise." She wiped her tears quickly, donning a mask of submission and sweetness once more.

Deep down, she still wished Ceanna would vanish—but truthfully, she hadn't started the fire.

"Alright, enough tears," Sirius muttered. "Look what I got you."


Chapter 9（最后一章免费章）
Like a magician pulling a rabbit from a hat, he suddenly held out a necklace—an ultra-rare, top-tier wolf fang pendant from a luxury brand. It gleamed under the dashboard lights, a piece worth seven figures.

Paige's eyes lit up instantly. "You remembered! I've wanted this for a long time, but it's been sold out everywhere!" She broke into a smile, threw her arms around his neck, rewarding him with a deep, lingering kiss.

"Haven't had enough, huh?" Sirius teased, half amused, half annoyed.

"Neither have you," she whispered back. "Or you wouldn't have brought me here."

The car began to rock gently, then more rhythmically as time passed. The deeper the night, the more intense the movement.

He had been with Ceanna for years, yet never crossed that final line.

Sirius had wanted to save her for something sacred—for the moment they officially bonded, when he would mark her to cherish the moment.

But Paige gave him a thrill he couldn't seem to quit. A heady, dangerous kind of pleasure he kept crawling back to.

A dangerous addiction he couldn't quit.

Far from the flames and betrayal, Ceanna quietly made her way back to the white cottage her mother had left behind. 


Ceanna didn't go back to the Lunaris Packhouse.

Ever since her mother passed, that cold, empty house had never felt like home. She was an outsider there.

Instead, she went to her mother's white cottage tucked away in a quiet grove of birch trees.

Every year on the anniversary of her mother's death, she and Sirius would come here.

They wouldn't do much—just lie on the wooden floor and talk for hours.

They used to take turns sharing stories about their lives, competing to see who could talk more.

Sirius would always end up making heartfelt vows in front of the old portrait of Yolanda Savage—Ceanna's mother.

"Yolanda, I swear to the Moon Goddess, I'll love Ceanna with everything I have. I'll protect her from the world. I'll make her the happiest Luna on this planet."

That promise still echoed in the walls of the cottage. But the reality outside those walls had fallen apart.

Her phone buzzed again and again, the sound crawling beneath her skin.

She didn't have to check to know who it was.

But her hand moved anyway and unlocked the screen.


Chapter 10
And there it was—a video of Sirius and Paige in the backseat of a car, tangled together.

The footage was grainy and clearly taken in secret, but the details were sharp enough.

This time, Allie didn't even howl in pain. She was too weak to scream.

Then a message came in, "Ceanna, Sirius and I are together now. All those times you called and he didn't pick up? He was with me. Today, when you were trying on your wedding dress—guess where he was? In the fitting room, with me.

"You saw it, didn't you? You saw how much he loves me. He can't get enough of me when we're together.

"Want to know when it started? Your 18th birthday. That night, he crawled into my bed.

"I've had five miscarriages because of him. You still think I'm the one who stole your man?

"I'm not the other woman—you are. If you weren't the future Alpha, I wouldn't end up in this miserable state."

What?

My 18th birthday?

Five miscarriages?

I'm the other woman?

H-how did things end up like this?

How on earth did I become the other woman?

"Mom, Sirius is a liar. He's been lying to me for eight years!

"It hurts. It hurts so much that I can't breathe. Somebody, please ... please help me ... "

She curled into herself on the floor, clutched her chest, and sobbed like a child. Her chest throbbed with unbearable pain, and inside her, Allie lay silent, barely alive.

The weight of despair consumed her.

She didn't know how long she cried—only that at some point, sleep overtook her.

The shrill ringing of a phone woke her. Morning sunlight streamed through the windows.

She picked up her phone and checked her screen. Over a dozen missed calls—all from Sirius.

As soon as she answered, his panicked voice spilled through the line.

"Ceanna! Where are you? I've been trying to reach you all night—I called you over dozens of times! Why didn't you pick up?"

"I was visiting my mom. I fell asleep," she replied coldly. "Did you need something?"

"I was worried sick! Just stay there. I'm coming to get you."

"I don't want to leave."

She hung up before he could answer.

The phone rang again. This time, it was Andy.

"Alpha, we've shortlisted several candidates for your contractual mate. The profiles have been sent to your inbox for review and selection."

"Got it."

...
Chapter 11
Half an hour later, Sirius's car pulled into the driveway.

He climbed out, arms full of grocery bags and boxes.

"Ceanna! Come help me—I bought so many things! I'm cooking lunch today."

He voice was light and cheerful, clearly in a good mood.

Ceanna didn't move. She stayed sprawled on the couch, motionless.

Sirius came inside, juggling the bags.

"Ceanna, look what I brought you!"

When he finally saw her tear-swollen eyes, his smile faltered. He dropped everything and rushed to her side. "Ceanna, what happened? Who upset you? Was it your stepmom again? I swear, I'll go settle the score right now—"

"It's not that. She didn't do anything. I just stayed up late working on pack business," she said quickly, grabbing his sleeve before he could storm out.

Ceanna didn't want any more drama. She was planning to leave anyway.

"Silly girl," he muttered, drawing her into a tight embrace. "Our packs are joining forces soon. I'm the man of the house now—just leave everything to me, alright? All you have to do is be the happiest Luna in all of Northmere."

Sirius pulled her into his arms with such desperation, as if the moment he let go, she'd vanish for good. His voice trembled, thick with emotion.

It reminded her of the fire long ago, when he'd carried her out of a burning building. He'd been just a boy, sobbing, shaking her as hard as he could.

"Ceanna, don't die! Please don't leave me. If I lose you ... how am I supposed to live?"

And now, she couldn't tell if the man holding her was still that same boy who once risked everything to save her.

"Stop this. I'm getting a birthday present for your mom. Isn't it coming up soon?"

Sirius reluctantly let go of her. He sat up and handed her a small box.

She opened it slowly. "Earrings."

"Do you like them?"

"I do." Her voice was quiet, emotionless. Her eyes remained distant.

"I'll put them on for you—"

"No need. I'm hungry. Let's whip something up," she said, dodging his touch and nudging him toward the kitchen.

He probably forgot how much she cared about fashion.

She wasn't just familiar with fashion—she lived it. She knew every major brand, every seasonal release.

This particular set was from the Soluna Collection.

The necklace featured a sun-shaped pendant. The earrings were crescent moons.


Chapter 12
They weren't sold separately. If you bought the necklace, you got the earrings as a gift. The set symbolized lovers—two halves of the same whole, bound by fate.

She didn't need to ask. The necklace was clearly meant for Paige.

What a joke. He claimed to love her with his whole heart, yet all she was worth ... was the freebie that came with someone else's gift.

And sure enough, shortly after that, she opened the livestream hosted by Paige. There it was—gleaming proudly around her neck.


Paige explained, "This necklace is a gift from my boyfriend!"

"Really? I heard there are only two in the entire world. Your guy must be seriously impressive. You can't just walk into a store and get one of these."

"Of course he is. To me, he's the best man in the world."

One of the viewers asked, "I also heard the necklace comes with custom earrings. MoonMuse, why aren't you wearing yours?"

"Oh, I'm just keeping it low-key. My ears have been a little inflamed lately. I'll wear them next time, promise."

Paige's sweet, sugary voice rang out through the livestream, chatting effortlessly with her fans.

Inside the kitchen, Ceanna glanced down at the engagement ring on her finger. The diamond sparkled a little too brightly under the light—so bright it made her eyes sting.

"What are you staring at so hard?"

"Paige is live right now. The necklace she's wearing is beautiful." Ceanna gave a faint smile and said nothing more. She put her phone away and walked into the kitchen to help out.

Sirius paused, caught off guard. He pulled out his phone and opened Paige's stream.

As the comments flew across the screen—most of them about the necklace—his expression darkened right away.

By the time Ceanna came out with pasta, Sirius was already outside on the phone, pacing in the courtyard.

With Allie barely hanging on to her life, Ceanna couldn't hear the exact words, but it was clear: he was furious.

"Do you like that necklace, Ceanna? If you do, I'll get it for you." Sirius asked awkwardly as he stepped back into the dining room. His voice strained with forced casualness, but if you looked closely, you'd notice his tail had slipped out, twitching with tension.

Ceanna smiled, her tone calm. "I like it, but I don't want to put you in a tough spot."

"A tough spot?"

Chapter 13

Sirius stiffened. His heart thudded, his face turning pale. For a moment, he panicked—did she know about him and Paige?

He was already scrambling to think of an explanation when Ceanna continued, still smiling softly.

"That necklace was designed by the top jeweler of our continent. Handcrafted, only two in existence, almost impossible to purchase. I don't know who Paige's boyfriend is, but he must've pulled some serious strings to get it."

She acted like she didn't know the story behind the necklace. She didn't want to expose the truth.

What was the point? She was leaving anyway.

"That necklace? How hard can it be? I promise—you'll be wearing it at my mom's birthday party two days from now."

He left without touching his food, muttering something about urgent pack business.

Ceanna didn't ask.

Whether he was really going to deal with pack matters or rushing off to see Paige—she didn't care anymore.

Once he was gone, she opened her inbox. Andy had sent over five potential candidates for a contractual mate.

She looked them over and picked the one who seemed least annoying. Then she hit "Confirm."

...

Two days later.

The Astral Pack's grand packhouse glowed under the evening lights. At 8 p.m., the birthday party was in full swing.

Alphas and betas from across the Northmere Continent had come to celebrate.

Paige was there, too.

Sirius had kept his word. Ceanna wore the necklace.

What he didn't expect was for Paige to walk in ... wearing the exact same necklace.

The moment Sirius caught sight of the necklace, his expression turned ashen. But that wasn't all.

Just minutes later, the heiress of the Cerulean Pack—famous for her family's jewelry empire—arrived, also wearing the very same necklace.

"There are only two of these necklaces in the world. Mine's definitely authentic," she said with a mischievous smile, eyeing Paige and Ceanna, determined to make a scene. "So, ladies ... which one of you is wearing a fake?"

All eyes turned to the twin necklaces gleaming under the chandeliers.

"Fake? That's impossible."

"Right? They're both daughters of the Lunaris Pack's alpha—there's no way either of them would wear something fake."

Whispers swept through the room like wildfire.


Chapter 14
Paige said with a knowing smile, her tone laced with implication. "If you're curious whether mine's real or not, you'll have to ask Sirius." People exchanged glances, picking up on the tension in the air.

Sirius's expression darkened instantly. His clenched fist trembled at his side, claws slipping out despite his effort to stay composed. His tail bristled, raised high, and his green eyes locked onto Paige with a glare so sharp it could cut glass. He looked like he wanted to rip her apart—right there in front of everyone.

Ceanna, ever calm, stepped in with a breezy laugh. "Oh, I think there's been a misunderstanding. Mine's just a one-to-one replica of Mr. Smith's original design—I had it made for fun. Guess it fooled more people than I expected. My bad!"

Her tone was light and playful. It was the perfect cover for Sirius.

She had known the necklace was fake from the moment Sirius gave it to her.

But she didn't say a word, didn't want to ruin Miriam's birthday or turn the evening into a scandal.

If there was anything she still cherished in the Astral Pack, it was her godparents, Miriam and Nigel Lowell.

"Happy birthday, Miriam."

Ignoring the stares, Ceanna walked straight toward Miriam, a soft smile on her face.

She took out a delicate emerald necklace—designed and crafted by herself over the past month. Carefully, she fastened it around Miriam's neck.

"This is Gleam of Selene. I designed it myself. I hope it brings you joy, beauty, and a long life filled with happiness."

Miriam touched the necklace with awe, her eyes gleaming. "This is stunning ... so intricate, so refined. You really made this yourself? You've always had such gifted hands, Ceanna. I absolutely love it."

The guests applauded, the room filling with admiration.

"Luna, happy birthday! I brought you something too," Paige chimed in, stepping forward with a dainty gift box.

"Thank you, Ms. Reed," Miriam replied with a stiff smile, taking the box without opening it.

"Ms. Savage," he began, "the payment for your cruise, the wedding, and even your contractual mate's one-month fee—all of it has been refunded. Please check your account."

"Why?" she asked, baffled.

"Because the Alpha King insisted on covering everything himself. You still don't know who your contractual mate really is, do you?

"He's the Alpha King of the entire Northmere Continent," Andy continued.

"When he saw your photo in our agency's client list, he signed up right away. We thought he was joking. I mean, why would someone like him sign up to be a contractual mate?

"But he meant it. That day, he was the most animated we'd ever seen him. Nothing like his usual cold, aloof self. He even brought up that ski trip you two took as kids—he remembered every detail."


Chapter 58
"We could all feel it—he'd been waiting for you for a long time. From that day on, he poured his heart into planning everything for you.

"Ms. Savage, I think you should believe in fate. If a man hurts you, maybe it's because he was never really meant for you."

When the call ended, Ceanna stared blankly at the long string of zeroes in her bank account. Her thoughts tangled.

She had even told Remus she would pay him double.

Now she realized—she'd been bragging about money to the Alpha King. How embarrassing.

Just as she was wondering whether to call Remus and explain, a headline on the news caught her eye.

"Homer Savage Kills His Wife, Sent to Psychiatric Facility"

The footage had already gone viral online.

But it was one line from Homer that echoed in her ears.

"I hate you. I don't want your charity. I'll drag you and your daughter down with me."

Ceanna's heart shattered at her father's words.

Why did this happen to her?

Her mother hadn't taken her own life and she had never tried to take Ceanna with her.

Even a wild animal doesn't harm its young—yet her father had tried to kill both his wife and child, all because of his own twisted insecurities.

Ceanna sat by the sea that day, crying until the sun went down. And when the tears dried, her resolve hardened. She would return to the Lunaris Pack.

The last thing she expected was to run into Remus again—this time in the first-class cabin of her flight home.

"What are you doing here?" she asked, startled.

Remus gave her a sheepish grin. "If I said I've been following you this whole time, would you believe me?"

He had followed her the whole time. Not because he didn't trust her, but because he couldn't bear the thought of her being alone.

Remus had only stayed out of her sight because he didn't want to annoy her.

"I believe you," she said.

As unbelievable as it sounded, she couldn't deny that the moment she saw him, her restless heart felt like it had finally found a safe harbor.

"Are you heading back for something important?" he asked.

"Yes," she said, her voice steady now. "For revenge."


Chapter 59

Ceanna chuckled playfully.

"Then take me with you," Remus said eagerly. "I can hold my own in a fight."

"Thank you for planning this trip and the wedding. I know you're the Alpha King, but it was my idea. I should be the one paying." She pulled out her phone. "Give me your account number. I'll transfer it now."

Remus smiled and held up his phone. "I like spending money on you. Please—let me."

He pulled up a QR code, and she scanned it to add him on WhatsApp.

Remus added her contact name as "Wifey."

Ceanna blushed and quickly turned to look out the window at the clouds 30,000 feet below.

How could she turn down someone so thoughtful, so remarkable?

But the scars of her past still ran deep. She'd lost too much before. And it had nearly broken her.

...

They checked into the most luxurious hotel in the Lunaris Pack territory.

Two bellhops rushed over to help with their luggage. Remus handed them tips with a friendly nod.

But suddenly, one of the bellhops lunged forward and grabbed Ceanna's wrist.

"It's you! Ceanna! You're alive—I looked everywhere for you. Why did you leave me?"

Ceanna froze, staring at his face in disbelief.

She never imagined she'd see Sirius again—much less working as a bellhop, standing on his toes just to meet her gaze.

"What are you doing? Let go of her! You've got the wrong person; this is my wife."

Remus stormed forward to grip his arm, nearly broke Sirius's hand.

Hotel security rushed in. The manager barked orders, and they finally wrestled Sirius to the ground.

"Sirius, stop it!" one of the staff yelled. "Ceanna is gone—she died. You need to get a grip. You're scaring the guests."

"No, no! That's her!" Sirius shouted, his voice cracking with desperation. "She's mine. She's my Ceanna!"

Before they could fully restrain him, he broke loose again and charged toward her.

With one sharp kick, Remus sent Sirius flying nearly 30 feet across the lobby.

"Are you okay, darling?" he asked, instantly turning back to Ceanna.

"I'm fine. Let's go."

She didn't spare Sirius another glance. Wrapping her hand around Remus's arm, they walked side by side into the elevator, laughing softly like any ordinary couple on vacation.


Chapter 60
"Ceanna!" Sirius's voice echoed from the floor. "Don't go! I was wrong—please, give me one more chance!"

He was pinned down by the security guards. All he could do was watch as she disappeared with someone else.

Sirius was fired on the spot. They told him he was never welcome back.

...

"Feeling a little heartbroken?" Remus asked softly, handing her a cup of warm milk. "The state he is in ... did it make you feel sorry for him?"

Ceanna lowered her eyes, uncertain.

She asked, "Was it my fault?"

"Of course not. Everything that happened to him today—he brought it on himself. None of it is your fault. If anything, all the pain you've been through was because of him. Sure, he owes you an apology, but honestly, I don't think you need it anymore. He never truly understood what he did wrong. He's just desperate now, regretting things because he has nowhere left to go." Remus sat beside her and gently ran a hand over her head, comforting her like a protective older brother.

"You're right," Ceanna whispered, her voice steadier. "I won't pity him. He made his bed—let him lie in it."

"I ran you a hot bath," he added, standing up. "Drink your milk, get a good soak, and sleep early. And as always—you take the bed, and I'll take the couch. I'll be right outside. You've got nothing to worry about—I'm not going anywhere." He gave her shoulder a light pat and stepped out, quietly closing the door behind him.

That night, for the first time in a long while, Ceanna slept in peace.

The nightmares that used to haunt her—the ones she couldn't escape—never returned. Because this time, she wasn't alone."


The next day, as soon as Ceanna and Remus stepped out of their hotel, Sirius stopped them again.

"If you don't back off, we're calling the cops," Remus snapped, clearly at the end of his rope. "I've told you, you've got the wrong person. What's it going to take for you to get that through your head?"

"Sir," Ceanna said firmly, holding out her ID. "This is my identification. Look closely—I'm not whoever you think I am. Please, stop following me."

"No," Sirius muttered, shaking his head in denial. "You're lying. You are Ceanna. You're her!"


Chapter 61
He stepped forward, his voice trembling. "Ceanna loves only me. She would never be with another man. This guy—he's just here to make me jealous. That's all this is." Sirius didn't trust the ID he saw.

His stubbornness had driven Ceanna mad. Without warning, she turned to Remus, cupped his face, and kissed him—right there, in front of everyone.

"Did you see this?" she snapped at Sirius as she pulled away. "He's my mate. Now please, do us both a favor and stop harassing me."

Still fuming, she looped her arm through Remus's and got into the car, never once glancing back.

Sirius stood frozen, disbelief painting his face. "N-no, that's not true. You are Ceanna. You're mine."

He wanted to go after Ceanna, but Paige came running from the crowd and threw her arms around him.

"Honey, please stop. Come home with me. I'm begging you—stop making a scene."

Paige desperately clung to him. She didn't care whether that woman was Ceanna or not—she was never letting go of her mate.

"Let go of me, Paige! You're crazy—I'm not your mate! I never was!" Sirius screamed, swinging wildly.

His fists rained down on her.

But Paige didn't let go. She clung to his legs, her body crumpled on the ground, coughing blood—but still refusing to release him.

From inside the car, Ceanna watched them struggle in the middle of the busy street—two broken souls tangled in a storm of denial and desperation.

She said nothing.

Remus stared out the window, quietly tracing his lips with a slender finger, still savoring the kiss she had given him moments ago.

"Sorry, I kind of used you back there," Ceanna mumbled, breaking the silence.

"Ms. Ramage, I was honored to help you," Remus replied with an effortless grin.

She blushed and turned her face toward the window, trying to hide her embarrassment.

"So, where to next?" he asked after a pause.

"I need to find someone who owns enough shares to help me take back what's mine," she said. "I want my revenge."


Chapter 62

"All right," Remus said. "What kind of revenge are we talking about?"

Ceanna looked straight ahead, her voice steady. "I want to reclaim my title as Alpha. And then I want Karen and her daughter thrown out of the pack. Let them live as rogues."

Ceanna had just one goal—she wanted Karen and her daughter to finally pay the price they deserved.

"That's an easy fix. Just marry me," Remus said with a cocky grin.

Ceanna gave him a playful glare. She jabbed, "I'll admit, you're good. But this mess involves politics between packs. Are you insane? I haven't even agreed to marry you, and you're already jumping into the fire?"

Even if he might not succeed, the fact that he was willing to try meant the world to her.

Remus shrugged, unfazed. "So what you're saying is, you're trying to save me the trouble?"

"Exactly." She smirked. "And let me give you some advice—don't go offering your heart to every girl who smiles at you."

"I'm not. It's just you. For you, I'd do anything. If you want to rise as Alpha, I'll be the one you step on to get there."

His words were firm, his voice steady. The way he looked at her—deep and unwavering—made something stir in her chest.

"You know, when you keep telling me that, I might actually start believing you." Ceanna's voice softened as she looked away, her bravado faltering.

"I hope you will believe me," Remus said gently. "Ceanna, just give me a chance. Let's try—three months. If you still don't love me by then, I'll walk away. No questions, no regrets."

He suddenly got serious, knelt down beside her, took her hand in his, and looked up at her with eyes full of promise.

Her eyes welled with emotion. "Then, will you come with me to the asylum? I want to see my father, who nearly killed me."

"I will. Wherever you want to go, I'll be right there with you," Remus said, nodding without hesitation, his smile pure and earnest like a child's.

It had been Remus who quietly hired someone to investigate Homer.

He was also the one who stirred up the media storm to put pressure on the man.

Whoever hurt his girl—no matter who they were—he'd make sure they'd pay.
synopsis: 在北默大陆，狼人社会中，月影狼族的未来女阿尔法西娜·萨维奇与星辉狼族的阿尔法天狼星·洛厄尔自幼青梅竹马，被月神指定为命定伴侣。然而，在婚礼前夕，西娜发现天狼星与她的继姐妹佩奇·里德有染，并得知他们已暗中交往八年，佩奇甚至多次怀孕。更令她心碎的是，她发现天狼星及其家族接近她只是为了她体内母亲留下的月核——一件能极大增强狼灵力量的宝物。西娜在绝望中拒绝天狼星，并假死逃离，通过契约伴侣机构与北默大陆的阿尔法之王雷穆斯·布莱克结为契约伴侣。雷穆斯多年前便暗恋西娜，他揭露了天狼星和佩奇的背叛，并帮助西娜复仇。最终，天狼星因背叛沦为欧米伽，星辉狼族覆灭；西娜的父亲荷马因杀害妻子被送入精神病院；西娜在雷穆斯的陪伴下，决定夺回属于自己的阿尔法之位。
synopsisEn: In the Northmere Continent, werewolf society is ruled by powerful packs. Ceanna Savage, the future Alpha of the Lunaris Pack, is engaged to Sirius Lowell, Alpha of the Astral Pack. They are childhood sweethearts and destined mates, blessed by the Moon Goddess. However, just before their wedding, Ceanna discovers Sirius has been having an eight-year affair with her stepsister, Paige Reed, who has suffered multiple miscarriages. Worse, she learns that Sirius and his family only value her for the Lunacore—a mystical relic left by her mother that can greatly enhance a wolf's spirit. Heartbroken, Ceanna rejects Sirius as her mate and fakes her death. Through a contractual mate agency, she enters a marriage contract with Remus Black, the Alpha King of the entire Northmere Continent, who has secretly loved her since childhood. Remus exposes Sirius and Paige's betrayal, leading to the collapse of the Astral Pack. Ceanna's father, Homer, who murdered her mother, is sent to a mental institution. With Remus's support, Ceanna resolves to reclaim her title as Alpha and seek revenge against those who wronged her.
